deeperExplanation: Abstract Base Classes (`abc.ABC` and `@abstractmethod`) define formal interface contracts in Python. Subclasses MUST implement abstract methods or raising `TypeError` upon instantiation.
internalImplementation: CPython checks `__abstractmethods__` set during class instantiation.

examples.explanation: Abstract Base Class enforces implementation contract.

debuggingWalkthrough.bugDescription: TypeError trying to instantiate class with un-implemented abstract method.
debuggingWalkthrough.incorrectCode:
class BadProcessor(PaymentProcessor): pass
p = BadProcessor() # TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class
debuggingWalkthrough.correctCode:
class GoodProcessor(PaymentProcessor):
    def process_payment(self, amount): print(amount)

bestPractices: Use `abc.ABC` when strict interface contracts are required across teams.
